ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: kyɛ yeli ko ba, “Yɛ yeli, ‘O potuuribo la wa zu a O eŋɛ tensogɔ saŋ, te naŋ da gbiri.’
translated: They said to him, "We say, 'If a person's pot is not good, he does not get good soup.'"

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: A bombie na ba naŋ da bore goore poɔ, la soba na naŋ woŋ a yelbiri, kyɛ ka a teŋɛzu yeltarre ane bombɔ nɔmmo maŋ fogle a yelbiri taa ka o koŋ wɔŋ bonzaa.
translated: The elder and the child went to the farm, and the elder told the child to wait, but he didn't listen and followed him, and when they got to the farm, the elder told the child to sit under a tree while he went to clear the weeds.

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Yelmeŋɛ ka N yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ mine be la kyɛ a naŋ koŋ kpi sɛre, kyɛ nyɛ a Nensaala Bie ka O waana ne O naaloŋ.”
translated: The elephant said to N, "If you are going, then I will give you a certain medicine, so that if you meet the Nensaala Bie and his brother, you will be able to defeat them."

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: A Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo mine naŋ da waa Faraziimine, naŋ da wa nyɛ a Yeezu naŋ laŋ dire ne yelbe-erebɛ ane a zuyɔɔ-derebɛ, ba da soore la a O potuuribo, “Bonso ka O laŋ dire ne zuyɔɔ-derebɛ ane yelbe-erebɛ?”
translated: The ruler of the land asked the wise men of the Faraziimi clan, and the wise men said that Jesus' power to heal the blind and the lame, they were surprised and asked him, "What is the source of his power to heal the blind and the lame?"

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Fo meŋ da kontoɔ̃ zo a fo gbaŋgba-tɔ ŋa nyonono, aŋa N naŋ da zo a fo nyonono lɛ be?’
translated: If your child is crying and you are not crying, can you say that your child is crying for nothing?
